description: reconstruct the full multi-line `docker run …` command for an existing container from `docker inspect` — volume mounts, --gpus, --network/--runtime, user-supplied -e env (image-baked env subtracted) — with secrets redacted, so deploy logs and Step 3 boxes show the real flags in effect
rules: read-only — runs `docker inspect` (and `docker image inspect`); never starts, stops, or modifies the container
subtracts image-baked env so only user-supplied -e flags are shown; falls back to a curated allowlist when the image can't be inspected
redacts secrets (NGC_API_KEY, *PASSWORD*, *TOKEN*, *SECRET*, AUTHORIZATION, …) with ***REDACTED*** so the synthesized command is safe to land in an on-disk deploy log
CONTAINER_NAME is required; degrades to a placeholder note when docker or the container is unavailable
output is copy-paste-correct (shlex-quoted paths, backslash line continuation)
usage: Set CONTAINER_NAME (the existing perception container, e.g. rtvicv-perception-docker). Output: docker-run.sh (the synthesized multi-line `docker run` command) under record_store.
limitation: Requires Docker and the named container to exist on the host; without them the perk writes a placeholder note and exits 0 rather than the real command line.
